body:
## Summary

Fixes #1625.

`--auto-attach-solution-summary` was silently skipping the solution summary attachment even when the AI session produced zero comments of its own. Observed on the external PR [Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP#1846](https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/pull/1846) where the session between [this start comment](https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/pull/1846#issuecomment-4270071583) and [this finish comment](https://github.com/Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP/pull/1846#issuecomment-4270114597) contained no AI-authored comments, yet the summary was still not attached.

## Root cause

`checkForAiCreatedComments()` in `src/solve.results.lib.mjs` returned `true` whenever any comment posted by the current GitHub user appeared after `referenceTime` — but the tool itself posts `🤖 AI Work Session Started`, `🤖 Solution Draft Log`, `🔄 Auto-restart`, `✅ Ready to merge`, etc. under the same GitHub account the AI would use for tool-call comments. Those bookkeeping comments falsely counted as "AI-created", so `--auto-attach-solution-summary` always suppressed the summary when the session started with `startWorkSession()` posting its marker comment.

Verified from the log file produced on the external PR:

```
[2026-04-17T17:45:46.739Z] [INFO] 🔍 Checking if AI created any comments during session (--auto-attach-solution-summary)...
[2026-04-17T17:45:47.403Z] [INFO] ℹ️  AI created comments during session, skipping solution summary attachment
```

## Fix — architectural refactor (per PR feedback [#4270296598](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1626#issuecomment-4270296598))

### 1. Centralized marker constants — `src/tool-comments.lib.mjs`

Every string that solve.mjs embeds in a GitHub comment is now a named `const` exported from a new `tool-comments.lib.mjs`:

- `AI_WORK_SESSION_STARTED_MARKER`, `AI_WORK_SESSION_COMPLETED_MARKER`, `AI_WORK_SESSION_RESUMED_MARKER`
- `AUTO_RESUME_ON_LIMIT_RESET_MARKER`, `AUTO_RESTART_ON_LIMIT_RESET_MARKER`, `AUTO_RESTART_MARKER`, `AUTO_RESTART_UNTIL_MERGEABLE_LOG_MARKER`
- `SOLUTION_DRAFT_LOG_MARKER`, `SOLUTION_DRAFT_FAILED_MARKER`, `SOLUTION_DRAFT_FINISHED_WITH_ERRORS_MARKER`
- `READY_TO_MERGE_MARKER`, `AUTO_MERGED_MARKER`, `BILLING_LIMIT_MARKER`, `MAINTAINER_ACCESS_REQUEST_MARKER`
- `LIVE_PROGRESS_SECTION_START_MARKER`, `LIVE_PROGRESS_SECTION_END_MARKER`
- `SESSION_FORCE_KILLED_MARKER`, `REPOSITORY_INITIALIZATION_REQUIRED_MARKER`
- `INTERACTIVE_SESSION_STARTED_MARKER`, `INTERACTIVE_SESSION_ENDED_MARKER`, `NOW_WORKING_SESSION_IS_ENDED_MARKER`
- `USAGE_LIMIT_REACHED_MARKER`

`TOOL_GENERATED_COMMENT_MARKERS` is **derived from** those constants, so changing a marker's text in one place propagates to both the post-side (comment body) and the filter-side (`isToolGeneratedComment`) — no second edit, no drift.

### 2. In-memory comment-ID tracking

Module-scoped `Set` records the GitHub comment `id` of every comment solve.mjs posts during a session, with two tracking helpers:

- `postTrackedComment({$, owner, repo, targetNumber, body})` — posts via `gh api repos/$owner/$repo/issues/$n/comments -X POST --input -`, parses JSON, tracks the returned `id`.
- `postTrackedCommentFromFile({$, owner, repo, targetNumber, bodyFile})` — same, for long payloads (log uploads).

Every tool-posting site was migrated to one of these helpers:
`solve.session.lib.mjs`, `solve.auto-merge.lib.mjs`, `solve.watch.lib.mjs`, `github.lib.mjs` (`attachLogToGitHub`/`attachTruncatedLog`/`attachRegularComment`/`requestMaintainerAccess`), `claude.lib.mjs` (force-kill notice), `interactive-mode.lib.mjs`, `solve.progress-monitoring.lib.mjs`, `solve.repo-setup.lib.mjs`, `solve.repository.lib.mjs`, `solve.mjs` (usage-limit notifications). Because every tool-posting path flows through the same tracker, coverage is uniform across all supported AI tools — **claude, codex, agent, opencode**.

### 3. Two-layer filter — `checkForAiCreatedComments`

- **Primary**: reject any PR conversation / issue comment whose `id` is in the tracked set (perfect by construction, never yields false positives from text matching).
- **Fallback**: for comments whose IDs were not captured (legacy posts), reject by marker substring match via `isToolGeneratedComment(body)`.
- PR **review** (inline code) comments are not filtered — solve.mjs never posts those, so any match is genuinely AI-authored.

## Test plan

- [x] `node tests/test-solution-summary.mjs` — **35/35 pass** (22 existing + 13 new for the marker module, in-memory tracking, `postTrackedComment` round-trip, and cross-module marker coverage).
- [x] `npm run lint` — clean.
- [x] Verbose log output includes the new diagnostics:

  ```text
  🔎 Checking comments by 'konard' after 2026-04-17T01:12:31Z (PR #1846, issue #none)
     ⏭️  Skipped pr tool-generated comments: AI Work Session Started=1
     📨 PR conversation comments after referenceTime by 'konard' (excluding tool-generated): 0
     📝 PR review (inline) comments after referenceTime by 'konard': 0
  ```

- [ ] Next real-world `--auto-attach-solution-summary` session on a PR with no AI-authored tool-call comments should now post the "Solution summary" comment.

## Docs

- `docs/case-studies/issue-1625/README.md` — updated to describe the new architecture (centralized module + in-memory tracking + two-layer filter) and list every migrated posting site.
- `.changeset/auto-attach-solution-summary-tool-comment-filter.md` — patch-bump entry with the full architectural rationale.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1625

…ary check

--auto-attach-solution-summary suppressed the AI's final summary even when
the session produced no AI comments, because the tool's own
"AI Work Session Started", "Solution Draft Log", and similar bookkeeping
comments are posted under the same GitHub user as the AI would use.
checkForAiCreatedComments() counted them as AI-authored and returned true.

Add a marker-based filter (isToolGeneratedComment / TOOL_GENERATED_COMMENT_MARKERS)
that skips comments containing known session bookkeeping text.  PR review
(inline) comments are not filtered because solve.mjs never posts to that
surface.  Verbose logging now prints which comments were skipped and the
per-surface count of comments that actually qualify as AI-authored, so the
same class of bug can be diagnosed from the log file in the future.

Includes unit tests covering each marker and negative cases, plus a case
study at docs/case-studies/issue-1625/ with the reproducing evidence from
Jhon-Crow/godot-topdown-MVP#1846.

Fixes #1625

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Introduces src/tool-comments.lib.mjs as the single source of truth for
every marker string embedded in tool-posted GitHub comments, plus
in-memory tracking of the comment IDs solve.mjs creates during a
session. checkForAiCreatedComments now uses the tracked ID set as the
primary filter and falls back to marker substring matching only when
an ID was not captured.

Every tool-posting site — solve.session.lib.mjs, solve.auto-merge.lib.mjs,
solve.watch.lib.mjs, github.lib.mjs (attachLogToGitHub, attachTruncatedLog,
attachRegularComment, requestMaintainerAccess), claude.lib.mjs
(force-kill notice), interactive-mode.lib.mjs,
solve.progress-monitoring.lib.mjs, solve.repo-setup.lib.mjs,
solve.repository.lib.mjs, and solve.mjs (usage-limit notifications) —
now routes through postTrackedComment or postTrackedCommentFromFile, so
every solve-posted comment is registered and filtered correctly across
all supported AI tools (claude, codex, agent, opencode).

Closes #1625.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: feat(solve): centralize tool-comment markers and track solve-posted IDs

commits.messageBody:
… CI limit

The centralized-markers refactor nudged claude.lib.mjs to 1506 and
solve.repository.lib.mjs to 1503. Compact the new import/log blocks so
both files stay within the 1500-line cap enforced by
scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: fix: keep claude.lib.mjs and solve.repository.lib.mjs under 1500-line…

title: fix(solve): centralize tool-comment markers + track solve-posted IDs for --auto-attach-solution-summary
